shortDescription: Unify video mode semantics and business rules in backend contracts; keep frontend thin and schema-driven.
objective: Move video-mode business logic into backend catalog/router/coordinator layers and keep frontend focused on rendering, input, and history presentation.
goal: Converge google/video-gen into a backend-defined capability model before any broader workflow expansion.

rules: Do not broaden into workflow scope until standalone video mode passes review and validator gates.
Treat backend controls schema and video coordinator metadata as the single source of truth.
Avoid adding new frontend-only business branches while this domain is active.

qualityGates: video business rules are expressed by backend schema or backend coordinator logic, not by frontend-only conditionals
frontend VideoGenView can switch among backend-defined video capabilities without hard-coded semantic drift
mode request, session persistence, history rendering, and preview URLs remain consistent for successful video generations
reference-image and extension flows are covered by targeted backend and frontend tests
stopConditions: no failed or blocked tasks remain in the active queue
video mode no longer relies on frontend-only business semantics for capability decisions
